seo.title: Workplace Programmes | M-Path men's wellbeing & allyship
seo.description: M-Path works with businesses through a DE&I lens — talks, workshops and support groups that give men targeted support with their emotional wellbeing and build male allyship.

hero.title: Bring men into inclusion and allyship
hero.lead: Feeling that DE&I doesn't include them is the main reason men disengage from it. We run talks, workshops and men's wellbeing networks that bring male colleagues into inclusion — trusted by Barclays, Nike, KPMG and L'Oréal.

items.label: Delivered across major UK & international organisations
A DE&I lens — engaging the group most likely to disengage
Trained facilitators who build men's wellbeing networks
Founder: BACP psychotherapist, ex-BBC journalist & author
Featured on the BBC, The Independent & The Telegraph

intro.body:
> Feeling uncertain about whether DE&I includes them is the main reason men say they either disengage or are not as committed to it as others in their organisation.
> — Forbes

For cultural change to truly happen within an organisation, a majority of colleagues must be engaged and feel included. Currently that isn't happening. We work with businesses through a DE&I lens — using talks, workshops and support groups to open up discussions, help male colleagues with very real emotional challenges, and address male allyship in the process.

steps.title: Healthy masculinity talk
steps.body: The talk engages the audience in challenging discussion about certain aspects of masculine expectation. It helps them think critically about their own behaviour and recognise that they have the power to help others overcome mental health issues, tendencies for violence, and the removal of female agency.

steps.title: Three workshops
steps.body: Three separate workshops — on empathy in leadership, men's mental health, and male allyship. Colleagues are challenged to think about their own difficulties in the modern world, the lived experiences of those around them, and how they can develop their powers of allyship.

steps.title: Men's wellbeing networks
steps.body: Many businesses have working groups and support networks, but very few have one specifically for men. As trained facilitators who've built these networks across a variety of business settings, we encourage attendees of our talks and workshops to take the bold step into men's work.

process.heading: From first enquiry to an ongoing network

steps.title: Enquiry
steps.body: Tell us about your organisation, your people and your DE&I goals.

steps.title: Scoping call
steps.body: We shape the right mix of keynote, workshops and network support.

steps.title: Tailored proposal
steps.body: Agreed content, timings and pricing, designed around your teams.

steps.title: Delivery
steps.body: In person or online, for audiences of any size.

steps.title: Ongoing network
steps.body: We help you establish and sustain a men's wellbeing network.

items.a: No. Sessions are **psychoeducational**, not group therapy. Our BACP-registered facilitators signpost to appropriate support where needed.

items.q: Will it alienate or blame men?
items.a: No — the opposite. We meet men with **compassion, not blame**, which is exactly why the men most likely to disengage actually take part.

items.q: Does it undercut our DE&I, or is it anti-women?
items.a: No. It brings the **disengaged majority into** DE&I. Building men's empathy and allyship benefits women, teams and the whole organisation.

items.q: Is it just a one-off talk?
items.a: It scales — from a single keynote to a full workshop series and an ongoing men's wellbeing network.

items.q: Can it be tailored and delivered online?
items.a: Yes. Every programme is tailored to your organisation and can be delivered in person or online.

note: Prices, further information and additional testimonials are available on request. Read more about founder [Chris Hemmings](/chris-hemmings).

cta.heading: Build a more inclusive workplace
cta.body: Let's design a talk, workshop series or wellbeing network that fits your organisation.
